ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315595); no specific prawn-and-pork shui jiao entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers filled dough products including dumplings; no prawn-pork-specific code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for prawn and pork dumplings; left null to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 8 dumplings (approximately 160 g), providing about 162 kcal, 13.9 g protein, 6.0 g fat, and 12.8 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Handmade Prawn and Pork Dumplings — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00010038). All per-100g values carry HPB provenance at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB entry; these are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork and shrimp dumplings (USDA FDC ID 172225) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for handmade prawn and pork shui jiao. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest analogous food: boiled wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi/wonton-type), which are reported at GI ~45–55 in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The wheat-flour wrapper is the dominant glycaemic driver; protein and fat filling moderates the postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (12.8 g) / 100 = 6.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100 g; potassium 82 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: With only 46 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 74 mg per 8-piece serving), these dumplings pose minimal sodium burden for hypertensive individuals, but dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chilli oil can add 200–400 mg sodium per tablespoon and should be replaced with low-sodium alternatives or used sparingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~48), moderate carbohydrate (8 g/100 g), negligible sugar (0.21 g/100 g), high protein (8.71 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycaemic index (~48) and moderate carbohydrate content (approximately 12.8 g per 8-piece serving) combined with 13.9 g protein support a blunted postprandial glucose response; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit to one standard serving (8 pieces) and avoid sweet dipping sauces that add significant sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100 g), low saturated fat (~1.2 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), moderate cholesterol (28 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low total fat, low saturated fat, and very low sodium profile make these dumplings a heart-friendly choice within a balanced diet; the moderate cholesterol content (approximately 45 mg per 8-piece serving) is well within the 200–300 mg daily limit recommended for cardiovascular disease management, and the prawn component provides lean protein without significant saturated fat loading.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6), a pro-inflammatory precursor
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pork is a source of arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid), which can amplify inflammatory cytokine production; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit consumption and consider prawn-only or chicken-filled dumpling alternatives to reduce pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Handmade Prawn and Pork Dumplings (鲜虾肉手工水饺) are a traditional Chinese boiled dumpling (shui jiao) sold frozen in packs of 8 pieces, commonly available in Singapore. Each dumpling consists of a thin, hand-rolled wheat-flour wrapper encasing a filling of fresh prawn and minced pork, typically seasoned with ginger, sesame oil, and soy sauce. The texture is tender and slightly chewy from the wrapper, with a juicy, savoury filling that delivers a balance of sweet prawn and rich pork flavour. Nutritionally, per 100 g the product provides 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium (46 mg) and negligible sugar (0.21 g), making it a relatively lean, protein-rich frozen convenience food. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 48 (low), driven primarily by the wheat-flour wrapper, with the protein-fat filling moderating postprandial glucose response. Clinically, the low sodium and low GI profile make these dumplings suitable for individuals managing hypertension or type 2 diabetes when consumed in standard portions and paired with low-sodium dipping sauces. The pork component contributes arachidonic acid and is a consideration for individuals with inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. These dumplings are a staple of Chinese home cooking and are widely consumed across Singapore as a lunch or dinner item, often boiled and served with black vinegar and chilli oil.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010038) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous entry and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous boiled wheat-flour dumpling data with clear documentation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific handmade prawn and pork dumpling product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010038) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for a directly equivalent product as of 2026. saturated_fat_g (1.2 g/100 g) and dietary_fibre_g (0.6 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork and shrimp dumplings (FDC ID 172225) as the closest approved-source analogue; these values should be replaced with direct measurement data when available. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on boiled wheat-flour dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Frozen product sodium may vary by batch; consumers should verify packaging label. Serving size of 160 g (8 pieces) is estimated at approximately 20 g per piece based on typical shui jiao dimensions; actual weight should be confirmed from product packaging.
